title: Pre-Construction Manager - Transmission & Distribution
description_text:
**Description**
  
 
  
The Pre-Construction Manager is responsible for the managerial leadership and direction of the Global Practice business line's major pursuits, including planning, schedule development, estimating, cost tracking, preliminary execution planning coordination, preliminary project and engineering management, leading constructability reviews and integration with the project execution team. This includes supporting the development and execution of business strategy, creating new enterprise accounts, leading/supporting commercial negotiations, developing high-performance teams, and promoting Burns & McDonnell's culture, values, and mission. The individual will also lead these teams in a dynamic and profitable manner based on Burns & McDonnell's core values of client satisfaction, commitment, dedication, integrity, safety, and overall outstanding service.
  
 
  

  
 
  
What you will do
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Responsible for determining pricing, cost tracking, preliminary execution planning, and cost and definition development for projects. This includes developing and implementing a system for reporting back actual pricing and labor productivities on active projects to influence ongoing estimate efforts.
  
 
  
+ Direct involvement in commercial negotiations across major and strategic pursuits.
  
 
  
+ Direct the coordination between Business Line Managers, EPC-PMs and CDB GP Manager.
  
 
  
+ Provide leadership between CDB Pre-Construction Execution, Burns & McDonnell Constructors, AZCO, and subcontractors to safely reduce installation rates, advance efficient execution, and continuously improve competitive offerings for business lines.
  
 
  
+ Provide leadership to the pursuits team; direct early project development and pursuit efforts ensuring effective utilization of staff.
  
 
  
+ Direct initiatives for Objectives and Key Results for business strategic goals across the Global Practice, including regional offices.
  
 
  
+ Provide leadership, development and guidance to the regional offices for EPC and major project pursuits.
  
 
  
+ Review, implement, and achieve business plans across the Global Practice, including regional offices.
  
 
  
+ Provide direction on business development efforts in the identification and vetting of prospective projects and clients.
  
 
  
+ Liaise between regional offices and applicable corporate initiatives.
  
 
  
+ Promote and manage internal relationships to establish guidelines and executable plans for conducting business.
  
 
  
+ Direct preparation and implementation of pursuit development plans to support and develop identified strategies for furthering the development of the business.
  
 
  
+ Provide senior-level technical expertise, input, and evaluation on projects across the Global Practice.
  
 
  
+ Provide industry expertise with respect to the competition.
  
 
  
+ Achieve established targets and standards to meet financial performance.
  
 
  
+ Present monthly financial information to leadership including sales and revenue forecasts.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Qualifications**
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Bachelor Degree in Engineering, Construction Management, or related field and 7 years direct program/project management experience required. Or,
  
 
  
+ Applicable experience may be substituted for degree requirement required.
  
 
  
+ Relevant experience in the electric utility industry preferred.
  
 
  
+ Prime contract and subcontractor negotiation experience preferred.
  
 
  
+ Experience in project cost and EPC pursuit develompent preferred.
  
 
  
+ Experience in managing projects, which includes proposal development, design team coordination & communication, budgeting, schedule management, and client coordination preferred.
  
 
  
+ Demonstrated success as to budget, savings, and/or profit margins preferred.
  
 
  
+ Strong understanding of all construction disciplines.
  
 
  
+ Excellent communications skills and supervisory skills of field construction personnel.
  
 
  
+ Expert knowledge of project management methodologies and tools including the use of P6, Microsoft Project or equivalent preferred.
  
 
  
+ Expert knowledge of estimating methodologies and tools including the use of major estimating platforms like InEight Estimate, HeavyBid, or equivalent preferred.
  
 
  
+ Must be able to meet company's driving requirements.
  
 
  
+ Computer proficiency in Microsoft Suite.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Compensation**
  
 
  

  
 
  
$150,000.00-320,000.00 Yearly
  
 
  
The expected compensation range for this position is displayed in compliance with all local/state regulations. The expected compensation range for this position is based on a number of factors, including but not limited to: individual education, qualifications, prior work experience and work location. The total annual compensation package will consist of a base salary and eligibility to participate in our discretionary year-end incentive bonus program.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Benefits**
  
 
  

  
 
  
Our extensive benefits package takes care of you so that you can focus on doing great work. From insurance and disability to time off and wellness programs, we provide the tools to meet your needs. As part of being 100% employee-owned, eligible employees participate in our Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP) in addition to our 401(k) retirement program. For more information, please visit the Benefits & Wellness page.
  
 
  

  
 
  
This job posting will remain open a minimum of 72 hours and on an ongoing basis until filled.
  
 
  

  
 
  
EEO/Disabled/Veterans
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Job** Estimating
  
**Primary Location** US-IL-Chicago
  
**Schedule:** Full-time
  
**Travel:** Yes, 100 % of the Time

title: Systems Administrator
description_text:
Why Bank OZK
  

  

  
 Founded on a legacy of more than 120 years in banking, Bank OZK is much more than just a company. We’re nationally recognized as an industry leader in financial services. That means we combine exceptional service with innovative technologies to deliver smart solutions to our clients across the country. We’re investing in small businesses, fueling economies in local communities and changing skylines in the largest cities across America. Here, we're not simply filling roles. We're fostering even greater careers. 
  

  
 
  

  
 The foundation for a great career starts with an exceptional team and a comprehensive benefits package. We believe in providing our dedicated team members with the best resources to support their physical, mental and financial wellbeing, including generous PTO, 401(k) matching, health, dental, vision (and pet!) insurance as well as special perks and discounts. Learn more about Bank OZK benefits (https://careers.ozk.com/benefits) . 
  

  
 
  
 
  
Job Purpose & Scope
  

  

  
 The scope of this job will include administration of the Bank’s IT infrastructure, including security, performance, and availability. This position will perform highly complex projects involving the design, analysis, maintenance, and implementation of the IT infrastructure. Responsibilities also include ensuring data and system security by protecting corporate information from unauthorized access, use or interruption; eliminating bottlenecks in the network; and identifying and resolving virtual hardware and software problems. 
  

  
 
  
 
  
Essential Job Functions
  
+ Maintains the bank’s IT infrastructure, including security, performance and availability as it pertains to VMware virtual environments, Microsoft Active Directory/Exchange and Microsoft SCCM.
  
+ Participates in IT projects involving the design, analysis, maintenance and implementation of IT infrastructure containing VMware virtual environments, Microsoft Active Directory/Exchange and Microsoft SCCM elements.
  
+ Ensures data and system security by protecting corporate information from unauthorized access, use or interruption by:
  
+ Research and recommend security measures to be included in the installation of new systems.
  
+ Monitor and evaluate data security implemented in the system; work to reduce all risks to corporate computer resources.
  
+ Ensure that the security program provides the necessary safeguards for good business practice and that it meets all corporate and government regulatory requirements.
  
+ Eliminates bottlenecks in the data workflow from applications to the physical/virtual network.
  
+ Identifies and resolves virtual hardware and software problems.
  
+ Coordinates software upgrades with vendors and bank staff as necessary.
  
+ Assists in planning and analyze information concerning job-processing time, virtual hardware/software utilization, CPU loads, system tuning, hardware tuning and virtual hardware integration.
  
+ Performs installation, configuration, and management of all virtual servers and appliances.
  
+ Performs upgrades Microsoft environments such as Active Directory, Exchange, and SCCM.
  
+ Assists in maintenance for VMware environment. 
  

  

  
+  Regularly assists the help desk with elevated tickets.
  
+ Follows Bank and department policy, practice and procedure while maintaining strict confidentiality and bank secrecy.
  
+ Establishes and maintains working relationships with end users, vendors, and managers.
  
+ Drives an automobile on company business.
  
+ Maintains nightly server backups.
  
+ Regularly exercises discretion and judgment in the performance of other essential job functions.
  
+ Maintains good punctuality and attendance to work. 
  

  

  

  
 
  

  
 
  
 
  
Knowledge, Skills & Abilities
  
+ Knowledge of storage hardware, physical server hardware, and software installation, along with testing and operations.
  
+ Knowledge of VMware and basic fundamental virtual hypervisors.
  
+ Knowledge of Microsoft Windows Server software.
  
+ Knowledge of local area networks and fundamental concepts.
  
+ Knowledge of the principles of data communications.
  
+ Knowledge of various software products such as Microsoft Office, Microsoft RDP, Microsoft SQL, Microsoft SCCM.
  
+ Knowledge of Microsoft PowerShell, and VMware power CLI.
  
+ Ability to work without close supervision.
  
+ Excellent customer service skills.
  
+ Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
  
+ Ability to work extended hours as necessary. 
  

  

  

  
 
  

  
 
  
 
  
Basic Qualifications
  
+ Bachelor’s degree in job-related field or commensurate work experience, required.
  
+ 3+ years of work experience with the software and systems referenced above, required.
  
+ Microsoft or IT certification, preferred. 
  

  

  

  
 
  
 
  
Job Expectations
  

  

  
 Operate customary equipment and technology used in a business environment, with or without accommodation. 
  

  
 
  

  
 Note: This description is not an exhaustive list of all job functions, duties, skills, and job standards required.  Other job functions, duties, skills, and standards may be added.  Management reserves the right to add or change the job requirements at any time. 
  

  
 #LI-KC1 
  

  
 
  
 
  
EEO Statement
  

  

  
 Bank OZK is an equal opportunity employer and gives consideration for employment to qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, and local law. title: Learning & Development Consultant
description_text:
About the Role:
  
As a CBRE Learning and Development Consultant, you will assist in managing the development, implementation, and administration of scalable learning and development programs that support business objectives. You will partner with stakeholders to identify needs, recommend solutions, and drive learning initiatives that enhance employee capability, performance, and engagement.
  
What You’ll Do:
  
· Consults with business units and subject matter experts to identify learning and performance needs, recommend appropriate solutions and guide content development efforts.
  
· Designs and administers training programs (virtual and live instructor led trainings, digital learning resources, videos, eLearnings, and microlearnings) and recommends delivery approaches that best support learning outcomes and business needs.
  
· Applies learning and instructional design expertise to address complex business challenges and develop innovative learning solutions.
  
· Coordinates, designs and delivers virtual and in-person instructor-led training across the US.
  
· Creates and applies evaluation and assessment tools to measure learning effectiveness, impact and alignment with business goals.
  
· Translates complex concepts into clear, practical learning solutions that meet the needs of diverse audiences and support learning and performance goals.
  
· Review training metrics, feedback and evaluation data to identify trends and drive continuous improvement of learning solutions.
  
· Manages communication and marketing plans for new courses and training programs.
  
· Remain current on learning industry trends, methodologies, and best practices and apply them effectively to program design and delivery.
  
· Develops strong relationships and solicit and incorporate feedback from business partners, operations, managers, consultants and trainers.
  
· Lead by example and model behaviors that are consistent with CBRE RISE values.
  
· Contribute to team success through effective communication and collaboration with teammates and partner team members.
  
What You’ll Need:
  
· Bachelor's Degree preferred with 5-8 years of experience in learning and development roles, training, instructional design or related roles. In lieu of a degree, a combination of experience and education will be considered.
  
· Experience delivering and facilitating training in-person and/or virtually.
  
· Ability to work autonomously within broad guidelines and adapt to evolving business needs and priorities.
  
· Ability to exercise judgment based on the analysis of multiple sources of information.
  
· Willingness to take a new perspective on existing solutions.
  
· Excellent organization and project skills to coordinate program efforts.
  
· Experience with utilizing multimedia authoring tools such as Adobe Captivate, Articulate Storyline and Rise 360 and/or other emerging technologies to design online content preferred.
  
· In-depth knowledge of Microsoft Office products. Examples include Word, Excel, Outlook, etc.
  
CBRE carefully considers multiple factors to determine compensation, including a candidate’s education, training, and experience. The minimum salary for the Learning & Development Consultant position is $85,000 annually [or $40.86 per hour] and the maximum salary for the  Learning & Development Consultant position is $95,000 annually [or $45.67 per hour]. The compensation that is offered to a successful candidate will depend on the candidate’s skills, qualifications, and experience.
  
Equal Employment Opportunity: CBRE is an equal opportunity employer that values diversity. We have a long-standing commitment to providing equal employment opportunity to all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, pregnancy, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, veteran status, political belief, or any other basis protected by applicable law.
  
Candidate Accommodations: CBRE values the differences of all current and prospective employees and recognizes how every employee contributes to our company’s success. CBRE provides reasonable accommodations in job application procedures for individuals with disabilities. If you require assistance due to a disability in the application or recruitment process, please submit a request via email at recruitingaccommodations@cbre.com or via telephone at +1 866 225 3099 (U.S.) and +1 866 388 4346 (Canada).
